topic: Instagram portfolio strategy — what to post

turns.content: ok so I want to actually take my Instagram seriously as an architecture portfolio. right now it's just random stuff and I need a strategy
A focused architecture Instagram can be a real career asset -- it's often the first place firms and collaborators check. What do you want people to think when they land on your profile?

I want them to immediately think "she does sustainable desert architecture and she's really good at it." like a clear niche not just generic architecture content. rn it's a mess tbh -- travel photos, food, random building pics, some old school renders. no coherent identity

"Sustainable desert architecture" is a great niche -- specific enough to be memorable, broad enough for plenty of content. I'd organize around content pillars: your own work (renders, sketches, diagrams), desert architecture in the wild (buildings you photograph), material and detail studies (mashrabiya, rammed earth, shading devices), and process content (your desk, Grasshopper screenshots, iterations). Maybe 80% architecture, 20% personal but with an architect's eye -- visiting a souk and noticing the roof light, your workspace during a late session.

yesss exactly. the personal content should still feel like it comes from an architect's eye. ok what about the visual aesthetic? I've seen some architecture accounts that are super minimal and cold and I don't want that. I want warmth because desert architecture IS warm -- sand tones, golden hour light, terracotta, shadow patterns

A warm palette is the right call and it will immediately differentiate you from the cold concrete-and-glass accounts. Establish consistent color temperature: warm neutrals like sand and cream and terracotta, accent tones of deep shadow blues and burnished gold, slightly warm white balance in your editing. Even your renders could be color-graded to match so your entire grid reads as one cohesive desert palette.

I mean that's so smart about the renders matching the grid palette. ok what about reels because everyone says they get the most reach but ngl I'm awkward on camera. like I don't want to do talking head content

You don't need your face at all. Process reels crush it in architecture: a screen recording of a Grasshopper definition generating a pattern sped up with a good audio track, hand-sketching timelapses, a slow pan across a render with a voiceover explaining the concept. Aim for 1-2 reels per week, 3-4 feed posts, and daily Stories for behind-the-scenes content. For hashtags, layer niche tags like #desertarchitecture and #mashrabiya with broader ones like #archidaily plus location tags like #abudhabi. Keep it to 15-20 max.
ok wallah I'm actually excited about this now. I'm going to spend this weekend shooting content around Abu Dhabi and start color-grading my old renders. first post by Monday inshallah!!

Abu Dhabi has untapped content everywhere -- the Louvre alone could give you a month of detail shots. One tip for your shooting weekend: golden hour is your best friend for that warm palette. Shoot morning and late afternoon and the desert light does half the editing for you. Good luck with the Monday launch!
